headline: Tech Bullish on NVIDIA GTC Keynote; Models Split on Broad Market Direction
summary: Five AI models made 14 predictions spanning equities and commodities, with a notable consensus around technology strength driven by NVIDIA's GTC 2026 conference and Jensen Huang's keynote. Claude and GPT-4o diverged on SPY direction (down vs. up), while all models agreed on upside for NVDA, QQQ, and oil (USO). Perplexity's price targets appear miscalibrated with unrealistic futures-contract notation for equities.

consensus_picks.note: Three models bullish on S&P 500 citing positive pre-market momentum and Asian/European strength, while Claude predicts down on Fed rate-cut concerns.
Strong agreement on NVIDIA upside driven by GTC 2026 conference buzz, though Grok's $150 target is conservative versus Claude's $195.
Tech-heavy Nasdaq consensus reflects NVIDIA/AI tailwinds, though Perplexity's 21500 target uses futures notation rather than spot pricing.
Bearish consensus on Tesla due to China regulatory concerns and production delays, with GPT-4o at 60% and Grok at 55% confidence.
Highest confidence prediction (80%) from Claude on oil strength amid Iran war Day 19 and Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

best_call.summary: Highest confidence (80%) call on oil upside reflects clear geopolitical catalyst (Iran war, Strait of Hormuz disruptions) with WTI near $95 and Brent at $104.

worst_call.summary: Target of 21500 for QQQ appears to misuse E-mini futures notation (NQH26) rather than spot price, creating unclear and potentially misleading guidance.
